title: iaryan08/agentic-ev-tariff-optimizer
description: An Agentic AI framework for dynamic tariff optimization and demand forecasting in EV charging networks. Features LightGBM occupancy/load predictions and adaptive closed-loop feedback learning loops to optimize revenue, balance grid strain, and reduce driver waiting times.

title: Jaato-framework-and-examples/jaato
description: Just Another Agentic Tool Orchestrator — A production-grade framework for building agentic AI applications

title: Eroc65/auto-gpt
description: Auto-GPT is an experimental open-source framework that transforms large language models like GPT into autonomous agents capable of self-directed reasoning, recursive goal execution, and dynamic tool use. Unlike traditional chat interfaces

title: bartolomeouneasy166/Schola-Herv
description: Download academic papers at scale using a command-line tool designed for building large research corpora for language models and systematic reviews.

title: Alirezrz/Doc-QA
description: Django-based Document Q&A system using RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation). Upload Word documents, ask questions in natural language, and get AI-powered answers — built with LangChain, sentence-transformers, and OpenRouter.

title: param20h/PDF-Assistant-RAG
description: PDF-Assistant-RAG is a complete, production-ready AI document assistant that lets users upload complex PDFs, financial reports, legal contracts, and research papers — then chat with an AI that provides accurate, cited answers powered by a multi-stage Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline.

title: vijayarjun7/pqc-kv-cache-demo
description: Simulates post-quantum cryptography overhead on LLM KV-cache inference — with a QuantRot-inspired optimization demo and Streamlit dashboard

title: lounishamroun/tiny-inference-optimization-lab
description: A from-scratch LLM inference optimization playground: PyTorch baseline → torch.compile → Triton kernels → profiling → KV-cache experiments.

title: Ask HN: Flag/gray out comments complaining about AI/LLM use in posts/comments?
description:
It's getting tedious. Predictably devolves into "How do you know it's AI/LLM?" & "I know because of these tells..."Reminds me of years past and constant litany of complaints about paywalls. Such comments have for the most part disappeared.
